title: DIVINE INTERVENTION | HG Amitasana Das | GITA LIFE Live
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: you
Thank you very much.
Thank you very much.
We are going through tough times.
So this session is all about how to get the mercy of the Lord.
Many times we feel our prayers remain unanswered.
It appears that the Lord is not listening to us.
And many times when our prayers are not answered or wishes are not fulfilled,
we at times even doubt the presence of Lord.
So all this we are going to discuss in detail in today's session.
So before we discuss, before we get into this discussion,
let us all together pray.
If your circumstances permit, you can kindly fold your hands.
We will be displaying the prayers.
You can also repeat the prayers along with me.
And then we will start the session.
Namaste.
Namah Om Vishnupadaya Krishna Preshtaya Bhutale
Srimate Bhakti Vedanta Swamin Nithinamine
Namaste Saraswate Deve Gauravani Pracharine
Nirvishesha Shunya Vadi
Namaste Saraswati Devate Gauravani Pracharine
Namaste Saraswati Devete Gauravani Pracharine
Namaste Saraswati Devete Gauravani Pracharine
Jai Shri Krishna Chaitanya, Prabhu Nithyananda, Shri Advaita Gadadhara, Shri Vasadi Gaurabhakta Vrinda,
Namo Mahavadanyaya, Krishna Prema Pradayate, Krishnaya Krishna Chaitanya, Namne Gauratushenamaha,
Namo Brahma Nadevaya, Go Brahma Nihitaya Cha, Jagadhitaya Krishnaya,
Govindaya Namo Namaha, He Krishna Karuna Sindho, Dina Bandhu Jagatpate,
Gopesha Gopika Kanta, Radha Kanta Namostute,
Tapta Kanchan Gaurangi, Radhe Vrinda Vaneshwari,
Vrishabhanu Sute,
Devi Pranamami Hari Priye,
Vanchakalpat Rubhya Shcha, Kripa Sindhu Bhya Evacha,
Patita Naam Pavne Bhyo, Vaishnave Bhyo, Namo Namaha,
Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna Hare Hare,
Hare Rama Hare,
Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare.
So today we are going to discuss three aspects of how we can go about attracting and experiencing the intervention of the Lord.
The first factor, the first principle is as you surrender, kindly make a note of it, as you surrender.
Krishna has given us a free will.
Lot of people ask if God is all powerful, then why bad things are happening.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
He could have forced us to do something.
the world could have been a better place to live.
So why do we see so much anomalies?
On one side we say that God exists
and other side we see lot of anomalies and aberrations
in what we observe, things happening around us.
So, Krishna has given us a free will, a choice
and He does not interfere.
In certain circumstances, in case of devotees, He does interfere
but in general, He does not interfere with our free will.
Krishna in the Gita says,
As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate.
The choice is ours.
If we surrender 0%, Krishna's reciprocation is 0%.
He is indifferent.
Although He is present in our heart as Supersoul,
He is witness to our activities and His sanctions, the reactions.
Yathamam prapadvayam.
As you surrender, so shall I reciprocate.
To give you a crude example,
a toddler, a child who is barely 6 months, 8 months old,
the lap of mother is completely dependent on the mother.
If by chance mother forgets, although the mother does not forget,
by chance the mother forgets to feed the child,
the child will simply cry.
It has no means to find the food by itself and go and take it.
And because the mother knows about this,
she is always conscious of the child.
She is always concerned, she is always anxious,
now my baby must be feeling hungry, I have to feed her, I have to feed him.
So likewise, pure devotees are dependent on the mother,
completely dependent on the Lord, completely surrendered to the Lord.
And as a result, the Lord also reciprocates.
The devotees try to serve the Lord and the Lord in turn tries to serve the devotees.
That is our constitutional position.
Sometimes we become little jealous that why we should serve the Lord.
We get a picture as if Lord is some kind of a cruel master,
who is demanding, just like in case of slaves.
He is demanding some kind of service from us.
Better you serve me, otherwise you are doomed.
That is not the scenario.
In the spiritual world, the devotees are trying their best to serve the Lord,
and the Lord in turn is making plans how to serve the devotees,
which is exemplified in the case of Arjuna.
Arjuna was asked, what do you want?
You want me or you want my army?
The condition being that I will not lift the weapons, I will not fight.
Because Arjuna knew the position of the Lord,
it was a no-brainer for Arjuna, he chose, I want you, my Lord.
And because Arjuna chose Krishna, he was completely dependent on the Lord,
he knew that if the Lord is with me, I don't have to worry about anything.
In fact, as we all know, Arjuna had Krishna by his side and he eventually won the battle.
Although in terms of army, in terms of number of soldiers, in terms of the generals,
Duryodhana and the Kauravas were far more powerful.
In fact, Arjuna was given, you want me or you want my army?
Arjuna chose Krishna, knowing very well that if I have Krishna, I have everything.
But Duryodhana, apparently, materially speaking, was wise,
and he said, what's the use of Krishna? Let me have the army.
So, because Arjuna chose Krishna,
he was completely dependent on the Lord, surrendered to the Lord,
Krishna reciprocated, literally in the sense that he served Arjuna
in the battle of Kurukshetra as Parthasarathy, as the chariot driver.
And we all know at all crucial times, critical times, Krishna intervened
and eventually Arjuna became victorious.
After the battle, when Arjuna was assigned the task of taking the queens of Dwaraka,
he was attacked by some dacoits.
And these dacoits were not some powerful warriors, armed with all kinds of emanations and weapons.
They were having some sticks in their hand, like cowards' men.
And they were attacked.
And Arjuna did try to protect the queens.
But unfortunately, they overpowered them.
And at that point, after having got defeated by these so-called dacoits,
Arjuna, there's a verse which says that, Arjuna is saying,
O Lord, this is the same Arjuna,
the same Arjuna who became victorious in the battle of Kurukshetra.
This is the same Gandiva bow.
This is the same chariot.
The same chariot in which Arjuna was seated in the battle of Kurukshetra.
This is the same chariot driven by the same horses.
In other words, all the circumstances were the same in terms of his abilities,
his strength, the chariot, the horses.
But he says, without you, I am helpless.
I couldn't do much.
So that is the position of the devotee.
In fact, this is the reason why a devotee is not proud.
Even if he accomplishes something big, he knows that it is all because of the mercy of the Lord.
He knows that it is not artificially psyching himself or sentimentally believing that,
no, no, no, sometimes people try to fake some humility.
No, it is not because of me, it is because of X, it could be because of something else.
They try to pass on the credit to their fellowmen, to their colleagues, to their teammates.
But Arjuna was very clear, being conscious of the Lord.
He said, it is because of you.
So when a devotee is completely surrendered to the Lord,
the Lord also accordingly serves his devotees, intervenes.
In case of materialists or people who are not at all surrendered to the Lord,
who are not at all conscious of the Lord,
Krishna is the witness and sanctioner.
This law of karma is acting impersonally, just like law of gravity.
If I throw a ball, if anybody else throws a ball, it follows a law, the law of gravity, it acts impersonally.
So law of karma also acts impersonally, in case we are not surrendered to the Lord.
But when we are surrendered to the Lord, then the Lord is not just the witnesser and sanctioner,
He is also the director.
He takes charge of the life of devotees.
Just like in case of, you know, when the bridge was being built to Lanka,
as per the law, the stone should be sinking in the water.
But the stones were floating.
So free will, we have the free will, the Lord does not intervene
in terms of how we exercise our free will.
The choice is ours.
If that free will is not given, then there is no difference between us and,
let's say, a programmable machine, a robot, or for that matter, a computer.
We have a software, we execute that code and this computer gives the output.
So there is no difference.
But we are not like robots.
Krishna has given us a choice.
If we voluntarily choose to serve the Lord, to think of the Lord, to depend on the Lord,
to come back to our original constitutional position, the Lord reciprocates.
So if we have to experience the intervention of the Lord in our lives,
it cannot be just by praying to the Lord in the times of difficulty.
There is a quote which is often floated around.
It says that if you are praying, only when you are in problem, you have a problem.
In other words, we cannot have Lord as, just like when we have something broken,
we take out quick fix, fevicol,
and we apply it, and once the work is done, the joint is made, we put it back in the drawer.
So we cannot use Lord as a quick fix to quick fix our problems.
At times we are disappointed that the Lord is not intervening.
So this relationship with the Lord, the surrender to the Lord, is a process
which we have to follow.
It's a path which we have to undertake.
And all the injunctions which is given in the Gita is to inspire us, to motivate us,
to tread that path, to depend on the Lord, to think of the Lord, to become his devotee.
Krishna in the Gita says,
mak chitta sarva durgaani mat prasadat tarishas
If you become conscious of me, although he does not force, he says,
if you become conscious of me, he could have forced us to become conscious of him, but he doesn't do that.
mak chitta sarva durgaani mat prasadat tarishasi
If you become conscious of me by my grace, how do we achieve God's grace, Lord's intervention?
If we think of the Lord, if we are conscious of the Lord.
mak chitta sarva durgaani mat prasadat tarishasi
atha chettu ahankaram
But if you act out of false ego, not remembering the Lord,
in our scenario, most of the time what so happens is we are completely forgetful of the Lord
and we remember the Lord only at times of difficulty.
Whereas Lord is saying, if you become conscious of me by my grace, you will cross over all obstacles of conditional life.
So,
to give an example of, I think we covered last last session, Draupadi.
As we all know, Dushashan and the Kauravas in the gambling match, the Pandavas lost.
They even at one point put on bet Draupadi and she was lost.
And Dushashan unfortunately in the whole assembly attempted,
because now the Draupadi was belonging to them.
They had all rights on her.
When you lose something, it goes to the other person.
So, Dushashan attempted to strip Draupadi in the whole assembly.
And Draupadi appealed, begged.
Looked for help all around.
Just like anybody in danger, we look for help.
More so women in this kind of a scenario.
She looked at her husbands, the five Pandavas imploringly as if,
please come forward, save me, protect me.
But since they had lost the match and lost her,
their heads were down in shame.
They couldn't come forward.
Then she looked at Bhishma Pitamah,
who is the Grandshire of the Kuru dynasty.
She even tried to implore Bhishma, please come forward and step and save me.
But Bhishma also was helpless.
Then finally she knew that if she has to save,
she has to depend on her whole strength.
And then she caught hold of the saree with both hands very tightly.
And on the other side, there was Dushashan trying to disrobe her.
And she was holding on to the saree very very tightly with all her strength, with all her might,
with all her ability, determined that she will not let Dushashan do what he is attempting.
But compared to her strength, he was very very powerful.
Physically well built, very strong, part of Duryodhan's army, one of the generals.
So Draupadi very soon realized that her strength compared to Dushashan's strength is limited.
So she begged the Lord.
She caught the saree with one hand and with other hand she raised her hands.
Surrender means you raise the hands.
Just like we have seen sometimes in wars, the soldiers surrender when they are defeated.
Surrender means to give oneself to the control of another person.
So still she had one hand holding the saree, the other hand she raised.
And tried to chant the names of Govinda, calling on Govinda to help her.
To help her in that situation.
And at that point she did not experience any of Lord's intervention.
Maybe the Lord was waiting for her to surrender fully.
Lord has wonderful ways to make the devotees experience his reciprocation
by forcing the devotees into circumstances where they are left with no choice but to surrender.
There is a very wonderful interaction, beautiful interaction between devotees and the Lord.
So then Draupadi realizing that she is helpless and she cannot protect herself, raised both hands, let loose her saree.
Stopped holding on and raised both hands and said Govinda, Govinda.
Completely dependent on the Lord.
And at that point the Lord intervened and supplied unlimited length of saree.
And on one side Duryodhana, Dushashana was trying to disrobe and there was no end to the saree.
And he could not do that.
So as you surrender so shall I reciprocate.
Draupadi surrendered fully, completely dependent on the Lord, not even on her might, on her strength.
And at that point the Lord intervened.
Now this is not a binary logic, 0 or 1.
I have also shared this earlier.
It is not that we have to surrender 100% and only then and only then we will experience Lord's intervention.
As you surrender so shall I reciprocate.
If we have surrendered even 10% there will be 10% reciprocation.
Although we can't quantify the reciprocation and surrender like that but we will experience some reciprocation.
The very fact that we are continuing in our journey in Krishna Consciousness,
the very fact that we are chanting, we are experiencing some reciprocation from the Lord.
So the more we surrender the better it is for us.
And if we want to experience the Lord's intervention then we have to surrender more and more.
We have to depend on the Lord more and more.
And in fact towards the end of the Gita Krishna is out of compassion,
knowing what is the best self-interest is saying surrender to me.
We all have created our own so-called responsibilities, duties.
We have weaved our own plans of protection.
Krishna is saying,
means give up.
Surrender to me.
Now,
does that mean that okay let's take a practical example.
Now we are going through COVID times.
You know we have lot of announcements being made, lot of guidelines being issued,
social distancing, wear a mask, take all the precautions.
Can we say that I am completely dependent on the Lord, I am surrendered to the Lord.
I don't have to worry, Lord will protect me.
I don't have to wear mask, I can go anywhere and everywhere.
I can open the temple and you know call all the people and have a mass gathering.
I know that Lord will protect.
I am surrendered to the Lord.
Can we do that?
Or for that matter if we become sick, can we say that I don't have to go to the doctor.
I don't have to take any medicine.
I don't have to take any supplements.
I don't have to do any of these things.
I am completely dependent on the Lord.
On the contrary, if I go to the doctor, does that mean that I am not surrendered to the Lord?
I am not dependent on the Lord.
Am I dependent on the doctor?
Am I dependent on the medicine?
Lot of people get confused.
I should not take vaccine.
I am dependent on the Lord.
We have that story, wonderful story of you know a priest.
There was a flood.
In the village.
And the priest was praying to the Lord for protection.
And then there was a weather forecast and a warning was given that it is going to be raining very heavily and please evacuate, evacuate.
But the priest did not listen.
He was there in his church and was praying.
And eventually it rained very heavily and the whole village was flooded.
And the priest went inside.
And even to the rooftop of that church.
And then lot of people were fleeing trying to protect themselves.
And even there were some rescue boats.
Rescue operations were carried out.
And the boats also came.
The priest said no.
And he was praying intensely to Jesus.
Please protect him, protect him.
The rescue boats came but he said no.
I don't want.
I am dependent on Lord Jesus.
He will protect me.
And even the rescue boats went off.
And finally the water level kept rising.
It reached to a state where the whole church was submerged.
And finally there was a cross on the top of the church.
And he was somehow holding on to it, praying to Jesus.
Oh Lord, please save me.
And even then he heard, he saw in the sky there were some helicopters.
They were throwing some, you know, rescue operations was being conducted.
They even threw some kind of a ladder so that he could hold on and come up.
But he was denying even that.
Praying intensely to the Lord.
Finally, unfortunately, the water level increased and the whole village was submerged including the church.
And he also died.
He went to the heaven being a very pious soul, devoted soul.
But he had a lot of complaint against the Lord.
This is the characteristic when our prayers are not answered.
We become very angry with the God.
So here also he was very angry.
He said, I prayed intensely.
I dependent on you.
I did all the things.
But why did you not protect me?
So Lord said, what can I do?
I sent the rescue boat.
I did the forecast.
I sent the helicopters.
But if you don't accept, what do I do?
What can I do?
So we should not see all these things which are happening as independent of the Lord.
A devotee may go to the doctor, will take the medicine.
Seeing it as part of the Lord, the protection of the Lord is coming through that.
He has to use his intelligence.
Prabhupada said, great devotees may not even do that.
Because they are completely dependent on the Lord.
They have complete faith in the Lord.
They are completely pure.
We are not in that state.
We cannot imitate that state.
Prahlad Maharaj was completely dependent on the Lord.
He was thrown in a pit of snakes.
He was completely chanting the names of Krishna.
Knowing very well the Lord will surely protect him.
But right now our faith is not that strong.
So if we were to do that, it will be an imitation without having that strong faith and dependence and remembrance and consciousness of the Lord in our heart.
It will be an imitation.
So devotee as a matter of duty goes to the doctor, takes the medicine but having the faith that these are just instruments.
Prahlad Maharaj says in the Bhagavatam, you may have the best of doctors, you may have the best of medicines but if Krishna does not protect, they all will be of no use.
Same way Arjuna says, the same Gandiva bow, the same Arjuna, the same chariot driven by the same horses but minus Krishna it is all null and void.
I could not even protect the queens from such dacoits.
Who are wielding sticks made of wood, wooden sticks, bamboo sticks.

So although a devotee will, will as a matter of duty go to the doctor, take the medicine but he has, sometimes when person goes to a doctor or medicine, they think that the doctor has saved the life, the medicine.
In other words, ultimate cause for their protection is the medicine.
The ultimate cause is the doctor.

That's why many times people even go and tell the doctor who have, you know, if they have gone through some major surgery and have recovered, they say you have given me the next life, you are God for me.
A devotee knows a doctor may have saved his life but the doctor is just a mere instrument in the hand of the Lord.
Sarva Karana Karanam is the Lord.
So credit goes to the Lord.
Due credit goes to the doctor.
Due credit goes to the medicine.
We are not fanatical.
Yes, doctors have a role to play.
Parents have a role to play in our upbringing.
We give due credit.
We give due respect.
Knowing very well that our Supreme Lord, He is the cause of all causes.
So Prahlad says, you may have the best of doctors, you may have the best of medicines, but if Krishna does not protect, all will be of no use.
And we are seeing in present times a lot of rich people, lot of affluent people with all the resources at their command, the best of doctors attending to them.
We have many celebrities who have lost their lives.
Just couple of days back, CBI director, 65 year old Mr. Ranjit Singh, he left the body because of COVID.
So many ministers, we have even in the BJP government, not because of COVID, even otherwise, Mr. Arun Jaitley, Sushma Swaraj, very eminent personalities.
Having the best of doctors attending to them, the best of resources, very powerful, very eminent.
In a ruling party, among the top five leaders ruling the whole country.
But the doctors could not save them.
So we as a matter of duty, that's why Krishna many many places in the Bhagavad Gita talks about, you have the right to do your duty, but do not be attached to the results of your activities.
As a matter of duty, yes, we will go to the doctor. As a matter of duty, we will take the medicine.
If we get protected, we give due respect to the doctor, give ultimate respect to the Lord, who is the cause of all causes.
It is because of Him, the doctors could make the right kind of medicine.
It is because of the Lord, the right medicine was available to you at the right time.
These days, a lot of people, in fact, hospitals, the beds are not available, ICU, oxygen, things are in shortage.
We thank the Lord.
So if we have to have intervention of the Lord, we have to be dependent on the Lord.
We have to surrender to Him. We cannot imitate that.
As a matter of duty, make whatever arrangement needs to be made for our protection.
And if those arrangements, the outcome is successful, we thank the Lord and give due credit to whatever instrumental causes are there in the chain of cause of causes.
So the second principle is,
the second principle is, go through Guru.
Many times, we approach the Lord and we directly go to the Lord, directly start offering prayers.
In the morning prayers which we chant, there is a saying,
yasya prasadat bhagwat prasado yasya aprasadan nagati kutopi
By whose mercy, yasya prasadat, if we get the mercy of a spiritual master, Guru,
yasya prasadat bhagwat prasado, we will get the mercy of Krishna.
Once, Srila Prabhupada, this was, you know, a devotee who was recollecting
memories of Srila Prabhupada
and he said that when Prabhupada was in Calcutta,
his grandfather had the good opportunity to have the association of Srila Prabhupada.
In fact, few mornings he had the opportunity to walk with Prabhupada.
Prabhupada would go for morning walk.
So his grandfather noticed that Srila Prabhupada was
very brave, he would walk with authority.
So he was very impressed with Srila Prabhupada.
So one day he introduced himself and requested Srila Prabhupada
if he can also join him and walk along with him.
So Prabhupada agreed and soon they became friends, they got to know each other.
So Srila Prabhupada mentioned to this gentleman who was grandfather of this person,
this person was sharing the memory.
The Srila Prabhupada said that my Guru Maharaj
has given me an order to preach this message in the western world
and I have taken his order very very seriously.
I am now his servant.
So Srila Prabhupada mentioned that
and he also said that, he told this person who is the grandfather of this,
this person who is narrating this memory,
he also told me that you also chant the names of Krishna,
depend on Krishna and whenever you have any anxiety, any fear, remember Krishna.
And he told me that Krishna Surya Sama, Krishna is like the sun,
when Krishna is there, there is no darkness.
I have taken up this mission of fulfilling the order of my Guru Maharaj.
Although I have no capacity, I have no ability,
knowing very well that Krishna will supply me everything.
If I am conscious of Krishna, Krishna will provide me everything.
I am completely dependent on the Lord.
So this elderly man finally eventually,
Prabhupada left Kolkata and you know he went to the west.
So time came when this elderly gentleman,
when he became little old,
he also was suffering from some kind of cancer.
He was hospitalized, he was in bedridden for many months.
And throughout he was remembering Krishna.
And every time he would chant the names of Krishna,
he would have the picture of Srila Prabhupada saying that
whenever you are in pain, whenever you are having anxiety, think of Krishna.
Krishna is like the sun, when sun is there, there is no question of darkness.
He says that is giving me so much of solace, that is giving me so much of relief.
Every time I feel pain, I think of Krishna, I think of Srila Prabhupada.
His face comes up, reminding me of the instructions what he has given me.
So if we want Krishna's intervention in our life,
we cannot directly approach the Lord.
We have to go through his confidential servitor, a Guru.
In the early days, Srila Prabhupada, he was doing everything by itself.
When he had gone to the west,
he was chanting in the Tomskin Square Park,
and then he had a small store fund.
He would invite people for the Gita class.
And soon some of those people who were chanting and dancing in the park,
some of them who became little attracted to Srila Prabhupada,
started visiting the store front.
And Srila Prabhupada would do everything by himself.
He would cook, he would make all the arrangements for the class.
And people would come and go.
He would even cook the Sunday feast.
Prabhupada, after the class, he would make a nice feast,
so that all the visitors could also get attracted to Krishna Consciousness,
through Prasadam.
Prasadam is also a wonderful form of giving mercy to the living entities.
So Prabhupada would do everything by himself, including cooking,
and these people would come,
they would attend the class,
they would eat,
and they would go away,
and Prabhupada would clean the floors,
remove those jhuta plates.
So one day Srila Prabhupada mentioned that,
if we want to get the mercy of Krishna,
we have to serve the spiritual master,
assist the Guru, the spiritual master, in his mission.
So some of them who had started coming regularly,
had become little serious in Krishna Consciousness,
sincerely practicing some of the things which Prabhupada was saying,
when they heard that,
they wanted to follow what Prabhupada was saying,
if you want to get the mercy of Krishna,
you have to assist the spiritual master,
assist the Guru.
By then they had heard that Prabhupada is the Swamiji,
and lot of people had started calling Prabhupada as Swamiji.
So one of them asked,
so they were very new,
so assist,
just like we have that perception of assistant,
so if a person is working in the office,
there is an assistant, executive assistant.
So one of them asked,
Prabhupada, do you work somewhere?
How do we assist you?
So Prabhupada said,
no, assist the spiritual master in his mission.
So then one of them asked,
how do I assist the spiritual master?
Do I have to give up my job?
Do I have to give up all my responsibilities,
and then assist?
You know, just like in a full time assistant.
So Prabhupada said that, no, no,
if you have to assist the spiritual master,
you have to assist in his mission.
And one way of doing it is,
you can give part of your salary,
hard earned money,
to the spiritual master,
to the guru,
to Krishna.
So then the next question was,
they were all very young and naive.
So next question was,
how to give money to Krishna?
Where to find Krishna?
Where to go and deposit it?
Just like, you know, if you ask,
if you have to pay me,
so you give the bank account,
you give the, you know,
FSC code and bank account number,
and you can do online transfer, okay?
Where to go and pay Krishna?
Where do we go and,
if I were to,
I agree to part with my salary,
some percentage,
if I can give it to Krishna,
where do I go and give it?
Where is Krishna available,
so that I can go and give?
So Prabhupada said,
you give to me,
I will give it to Krishna.
And then Prabhupada explained,
that a genuine Guru
is a confidential servitor of Krishna.
